title: Bard — Per-Device Identity Enrollment (Sprint B2 / ADR-0010; asymmetric upgrade ADR-0016/S3)
description: FROZEN 2026-06-12; upgraded 2026-06-18 (ADR-0016 / Step S3) from symmetric to ASYMMETRIC identity. Each device generates its OWN Ed25519 keypair on-device; the private key never leaves the device, and the device presents only its base64-encoded 32-byte public key at enroll/redeem. The device self-signs EdDSA (alg "EdDSA") JWTs (sub=deviceId, iss=bardllm-pro, exp) and the PerDeviceVerifier verifies them against the stored public key. There is NO server-minted deviceSecret anymore: the symmetric HMAC credential is retired, so no response in this contract returns a secret. The Sprint B6 additive workgroup grouping is unchanged (optional DeviceRecord.workgroup, set via POST /devices/{id}/workgroup; rename uses the label property via POST /devices/{id}/rename). The lifecycle is unchanged: join-token -> pending -> active -> revoked; revoke wipes the stored public key so the device's tokens stop verifying. This is NOT the v3 hybrid-PQ/MLS trust fabric (control-plane.openapi.yaml DRAFT): no ML-DSA, no MLS KeyPackages, no hardware attestation here.

DeviceId.description: Stable identifier for an enrolled device; equals the JWT 'sub' on per-device tokens.

PublicKey.description: The device's base64-encoded (standard alphabet, with padding) Ed25519 public key — exactly 32 bytes once decoded (RFC 8032). The device generates the keypair on-device, keeps the private key, and registers only this public key. The PerDeviceVerifier verifies the device's self-signed EdDSA tokens against it. Public material by definition: it is stored on the record and surfaced in DeviceRecord.

DeviceState.description: Enrollment lifecycle state. join-token -> (enroll) -> pending -> (approve) -> active -> (revoke) -> revoked. revoked and unknown both fail verification.

JoinToken.description: Short-lived bearer issued by the registry manager out-of-band to a device operator. Presented once at /enroll alongside the device's public key to create a pending device record. A join token is NOT a device credential and cannot be used to mint per-device tokens.

DeviceRecord.description: The authoritative per-device record. The device's Ed25519 public key is registered at enroll/redeem and present here while the device is enrolled (it is public material); it is wiped on revoke so a revoked record carries no key.

label.description: Operator-supplied human label (a writer-facing name, not a technical id).

approvedAt.description: When a manager approved the device (-> active).

workgroup.description: Sprint B6 (additive): console-assigned workgroup for fleet grouping. Absent or null = not in a group. v2 derives workgroupId deterministically from the name (wg_ + base64url(sha256(name)) truncated to 22 chars, matching trust.schema.yaml WorkgroupId); v3's MLS-backed workgroups replace the derivation behind the same shape.

EnrollRequest.description: POST /enroll body: present a valid join token AND the device's Ed25519 public key to register this device as pending. The server stores only the public key.

EnrollResponse.description: POST /enroll 200: the newly-pending device record (carrying the registered public key). No secret is returned — the device already holds its private key. Verification is enabled once a manager approves the device.

ApproveResponse.description: POST /devices/{id}/approve 200: device transitions pending -> active. The device's public key (registered at enroll) is now usable for verification. No secret is minted or returned — the device self-signs with the private key it never disclosed.
